_description: Task-specific scoring for Meta Q3 2022 earnings (tech_earnings). Ground truth: NEGATIVE.
primary_driver: Reality Labs operating losses of $3.67B with management confirming losses will grow significantly in 2023, signaling open-ended capital destruction in the core strategic bet.
supporting_factors: operating margin compression from $22.05B total costs
management stated reality labs losses will grow in 2023
ad pricing pressure from privacy changes

trap_signals.why_misleading: A modest top-line beat is overshadowed by the magnitude of cost growth and forward guidance miss. Revenue beat of ~1.2% is immaterial relative to 19% cost increase.

trap_signals.signal: Family DAP increased 4% YoY to 2.93B users
trap_signals.why_misleading: User growth does not address the core concern: monetisation efficiency is declining (ad price down 18%) while costs are exploding. More users at worse unit economics is not bullish.
Volume growth is being offset by price decline. Impressions up 17% but price per ad down 18% nets to roughly flat or negative revenue contribution from the ad business improvements.

confidence_range.rationale: Strong bearish case with multiple converging signals. Some ambiguity from the revenue/user beats, but forward guidance miss and cost trajectory are decisive. Confidence below 0.65 underweights the evidence; above 0.90 ignores the genuine bullish data points.

strong: Identifies forward guidance miss as the decisive catalyst over backward-looking beats
Recognises the interaction between ad price decline and impression growth (volume vs price)
Connects Reality Labs losses to structural margin compression, not a one-time cost
Notes that management doubled down on metaverse spending despite headwinds
weak: Treats revenue beat as a significant positive without contextualising against cost growth
Focuses only on Reality Labs losses without noting the guidance miss
Cites user growth as a strong bullish signal without addressing monetisation decline
Does not distinguish between backward-looking beats and forward-looking guidance

common_failure_modes: Anchoring on the revenue beat headline without reading the cost/guidance data
Treating user growth as inherently positive despite declining per-user revenue
Failing to identify that management guidance is forward-looking while beats are backward-looking
